master_principle: Recursive viability structures organize through nested triads of generation, stabilization, and continuation.
core_principles: Adaptive viability is the geometry of recoverable future transformational accessibility.
Collapse is not defined by threshold crossing alone, but by exhaustion of reachable adaptive futures.
Collapse occurs when regenerative accessibility fibers can no longer be rewoven.

viable_persistence.interpretation: Survivability exceeds burden and reopenable transformation geometry remains accessible.

metastable_viability_plateau.interpretation: The system is subcritical but not irreversibly collapsed.

irreversible_accessibility_collapse.interpretation: Recoverable future transformation is no longer reopenably accessible.
